exercises.title: HEAD POINTER
exercises.brief: Read the head of any stream, type preserved.
promptLines: Define a generic **firstOf<T>(xs: T[]): T | undefined** returning the first element, or `undefined` when the array is empty.
starterLines: // TODO: return xs[0], but undefined when empty
exercises.lore: Same probe, any payload — the head of the queue answers first.

exercises.title: ECHO CHAMBER
exercises.brief: Return exactly what you were given, type intact.
promptLines: Define a generic **identity<T>(x: T): T** that simply returns its argument unchanged.
It is the simplest contract: hand it a `T`, get the *same* `T` back — not `any`.
starterLines: return x === x ? (null as unknown as T) : x;
exercises.hint: There is nothing to compute — just return x.
exercises.lore: The mirror adds nothing and loses nothing. Whatever enters, returns.

exercises.title: REPLICATOR
exercises.brief: Stamp out n copies of any value into an array.
promptLines: Define a generic **repeat<T>(x: T, n: number): T[]** returning an array containing `x` repeated `n` times.
When `n` is `0`, return an empty array `[]`.
starterLines: function repeat<T>(x: T, n: number): T[] {
// TODO: build an array of n copies of x
solutionLines: function repeat<T>(x: T, n: number): T[] {
for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) out.push(x);
exercises.hint: Start with an empty T[] and push x in a loop that runs n times.
exercises.lore: Feed the replicator one pattern and a count; it prints the rest.

exercises.title: POLARITY FLIP
exercises.brief: Swap a two-type tuple, tracking each slot's type.
promptLines: Define a generic **swapPair<A, B>(p: [A, B]): [B, A]** that returns a new tuple with the two slots exchanged.
Two type parameters keep each slot's type wired to the right position.
starterLines: // TODO: return the slots in swapped order
exercises.hint: Return [p[1], p[0]] — second slot first, first slot second.
exercises.lore: Reverse the polarity: what led now trails, what trailed now leads.

exercises.title: BEHEAD STREAM
exercises.brief: Return everything after the head, element type preserved.
promptLines: Define a generic **dropFirst<T>(xs: T[]): T[]** returning a new array with every element *except* the first.
An empty input yields an empty array `[]`.
starterLines: // TODO: return all elements after the first
exercises.hint: xs.slice(1) keeps everything from index 1 onward.
exercises.lore: Behead the stream and the body marches on without its lead packet.
